title: Houses for Rent in Broadway-Slavic Village, Cleveland
metaTitle: Houses for Rent in Broadway-Slavic Village, OH
metaDescription: Houses for rent in Broadway-Slavic Village, Cleveland (44105) — rent ranges, transit, and Section 8-friendly homes. Book a showing.
primaryKeyword: houses for rent Broadway-Slavic Village cleveland
answerBox: Broadway-Slavic Village is the historic core of Cleveland's Slavic Village area, in ZIP code 44105 on the city's southeast side. Rent Finder Cleveland manages voucher-friendly rental homes in and around this neighborhood, and every home we manage accepts Section 8 and is HUD-inspection-ready.

sections.h2: Renting a house in Broadway-Slavic Village
body: Broadway-Slavic Village is the official City Planning Commission name for the historic core of the Slavic Village neighborhood, centered on the **Broadway Avenue** business corridor on Cleveland's southeast side. The area was historically settled by Polish and Czech immigrant communities, and much of its housing stock reflects that early-20th-century development pattern of modest single-family homes and duplexes.

This is one of the neighborhoods where **Rent Finder Cleveland** manages Section 8-friendly rental homes. Every home we manage — house, duplex, or fourplex — accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. [Book a free showing](/p/book-showing) to see what's currently available, or [start an application](/apply) if you've already found a home you like.

sections.h2: What does it cost to rent a house here?
body: Cleveland-wide, three-bedroom homes are running around $1,350 a month and two-bedroom homes around $1,100, per Zumper's July 2026 rent report. Our own portfolio across Cleveland spans roughly $700 to $1,800 a month, with a typical rent near $1,000 — and homes in ZIP 44105, which covers Broadway-Slavic Village and neighboring Union-Miles, tend to fall toward the more affordable end of that range.

The neighborhood sits along Broadway Avenue, a historic commercial corridor, with additional shopping along Fleet Avenue. Multiple **RTA bus routes** connect the area to downtown Cleveland, and Interstate 77 and Interstate 490 both run nearby, making it straightforward to reach downtown or the southern suburbs by car.

items: **Transit:** Multiple RTA bus routes to downtown

sections.h2: Section 8 and Housing Choice Vouchers here
body: Every home Rent Finder Cleveland manages — in this neighborhood and across our portfolio — accepts Section 8 and is HUD-inspection-ready. Voucher holders apply through the **Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority (CMHA)**; our [Section 8 housing guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) walks through the application, waitlist, and inspection process.

Keep in mind that Ohio has no statewide source-of-income protection law, and Cleveland itself does not currently have a voucher-nondiscrimination ordinance as of July 2026, so always confirm a landlord's policy directly.

If Broadway-Slavic Village doesn't have what you need right now, our team also manages homes in nearby Southeast Cleveland areas. See our guides to [houses for rent in Slavic Village](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-slavic-village-cleveland/), [houses for rent in Buckeye](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-buckeye-cleveland/), and [houses for rent in Union-Miles](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-union-miles-cleveland/), or browse our full [Cleveland houses for rent](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) hub.

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland have rental homes in Broadway-Slavic Village?
faq.a: Yes. We manage voucher-friendly rental homes in and around Broadway-Slavic Village (ZIP 44105) as part of our broader Southeast Cleveland portfolio. Availability changes regularly, so book a free showing or tell our team what you're looking for and we'll point you to current options.

faq.q: How much does it cost to rent a house in this ZIP code?
faq.a: Citywide, Zumper's July 2026 report shows two-bedroom homes averaging about $1,100 a month and three-bedroom homes about $1,350. Our own Cleveland portfolio ranges from about $700 to $1,800 a month with a typical rent near $1,000, and 44105-area homes tend toward the more affordable end.

faq.q: Are Broadway-Slavic Village rentals Section 8 friendly?
faq.a: Every home Rent Finder Cleveland manages accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready, including homes in this neighborhood. Voucher holders apply through CMHA, Cuyahoga County's public housing authority, which accepts preliminary applications year-round. See our Section 8 guide for the full application, waitlist, and inspection process.

faq.q: What ZIP code is Broadway-Slavic Village in?
faq.a: Broadway-Slavic Village is in ZIP code 44105 on Cleveland's southeast side. It's the historic core of the larger Slavic Village area, centered on the Broadway Avenue business corridor, and neighbors Union-Miles to the south, near Interstate 77 and Interstate 490.

faq.q: What's the housing stock like in Broadway-Slavic Village?
faq.a: The neighborhood was historically settled by Polish and Czech immigrant communities, and much of the housing stock dates to that early-20th-century development — mostly modest single-family homes and duplexes with front porches and small yards, typical of Cleveland's older southeast-side neighborhoods.
